title: Ongoing Monitoring & Review Policies
summary: Organisational policies require ongoing monitoring and periodic review of AI risks, including data retention and accountability mechanisms.
detail: Use this reference when a requirement needs governance ownership, review cadence, accountability policies, risk monitoring, escalation paths, or lifecycle maintenance. It is useful for turning one-off technical controls into sustained organizational practice.

title: Mission & Context Documentation
summary: AI risks are mapped to system mission, context of use, and stakeholders, including documentation of data sources and known limitations.
detail: Use this reference when a requirement should connect the AI system to its intended purpose, deployment context, stakeholders, data sources, assumptions, and limitations. In healthcare AI, it helps ensure requirements are grounded in the actual clinical setting rather than a generic model description.

title: AI System Performance & Validity
summary: AI system performance is measured and demonstrated against representative deployment conditions and stakeholder needs.
detail: Use this reference when a requirement should specify validation metrics, test conditions, representative datasets, performance thresholds, and evidence that the system works for the intended users and setting. It supports clinical validation, monitoring, and re-evaluation after changes.

title: Fairness & Bias Evaluation
summary: Fairness and bias of AI outputs are evaluated, documented, and communicated, including across demographic subgroups.
detail: Use this reference when a requirement should include subgroup analysis, fairness metrics, bias testing, documentation of findings, communication of limitations, and mitigation follow-up. For healthcare AI, it supports checking whether model behavior differs across patient populations.
